seo.title: Smolagents Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Smolagents agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams adopting Smolagents face heightened risks due to the framework's lightweight design, which prioritizes simplicity but often lacks built-in safeguards against unsafe code execution or mishandled PHI. Non-compliance with HIPAA, HITECH, and FDA regulations can lead to patient harm, fines up to $1.5M per violation, and loss of licenses. Vex provides essential guardrails by verifying agent outputs for data leakage, auditability, and regulatory adherence before execution.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits unauthorized disclosure of Protected Health Information (PHI) and requires patient data to remain within secure environments.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent-generated code and outputs for PHI leakage, enforcing de-identification before LLM processing and blocking data exfiltration attempts.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records and signatures be trustworthy, reliable, and auditable for AI-assisted medical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent reasoning steps with timestamps and hashes for full audit trails, ensuring every decision is traceable and verifiable.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notification requirements and higher penalties for PHI mishandling.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects potential breaches in real-time, alerts on unsafe code patterns, and prevents execution of non-compliant agent actions.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls over security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces runtime policies for secure code execution, input validation, and error handling, providing evidence for SOC 2 audits.

industryRisks.name: PHI Data Leakage via Unsafe Code
industryRisks.description: Smolagents generating Python code that logs or transmits unredacted patient data to external APIs.
industryRisks.example: Agent writes `print(patient_ssn)` or calls unsecured logging service with PHI.

industryRisks.name: Multi-Step Reasoning PHI Compounding
industryRisks.description: Errors accumulate across agent steps, progressively exposing de-identified PHI through faulty logic.
industryRisks.example: Step 1 de-identifies names but Step 3 reconstructs identity via zip code + birthdate correlations.

industryRisks.name: Unauditable Medical Decision Paths
industryRisks.description: Minimal error handling obscures reasoning chains needed for FDA compliance.
industryRisks.example: Agent recommends dosage but fails silently on edge cases, hiding calculation errors.

industryRisks.name: Tool Misinterpretation of Clinical Data
industryRisks.description: Agents misread tool descriptions, applying wrong clinical protocols or dosages.
industryRisks.example: Confuses 'mg/kg' pediatric dosing tool with adult flat-rate dosing.

implementation.title: Wrap Smolagents with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protect against unsafe code execution by verifying all agent.run() outputs before execution.

implementation.title: PHI De-identification Guardrail
implementation.description: Enforce automatic PHI scrubbing before agent processing with Vex policies.
implementation.code:
import vex
import re
from smolagents import Agent

PHI_PATTERNS = [
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',  # SSN
    r'\b[A-Z][a-z]+\s[A-Z][a-z]+\b',  # Names
    r'\b\d{5}\b'  # ZIP
]

def preprocess_phi(text):
    verified = vex.verify(
        text,
        policies=["phi_deidentification_required"],
        custom_checks=[
            lambda t: all(not re.search(p, t) for p in PHI_PATTERNS)
        ]
    )
    return "[PHI REDACTED]" if not verified else text

agent = Agent()
prompt = preprocess_phi(patient_record)
result = agent.run(prompt)

implementation.title: Audit Trail for Medical Decisions
implementation.description: Capture complete reasoning chains for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.
implementation.code:
import vex
import json
from smolagents import MultiAgent

class AuditableSmolAgent(MultiAgent):
    def run(self, prompt):
        # Capture full execution trace
        trace = {
            "timestamp": vex.now(),
            "prompt": prompt,
            "steps": [],
            "hash": vex.content_hash(prompt)
        }
        
        result = super().run(prompt)
        trace["steps"] = self.reasoning_history
        trace["decision_hash"] = vex.content_hash(str(result))
        
        # Vex audit verification
        vex.verify(
            trace,
            policies=["fda_audit_trail", "complete_reasoning"],
            metadata={"compliance": "21cfr11"}
        )
        
        vex.log_audit(trace)  # Persist for compliance
        return result

clinical_agent = AuditableSmolAgent(tools=medical_tools)

caseStudyScenario.heading: Smolagents in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized clinic deploys Smolagents to automate pediatric dosage calculations from EHR data. The lightweight framework processes patient weight, age, and medication protocols to generate Python code for precise mg/kg dosing. However, without guardrails, the code agent begins generating scripts that inadvertently log full patient records—including SSNs and names—to debug files during multi-step reasoning. 

In parallel, a tool agent misinterprets the 'calculate_dosage' tool description, confusing pediatric protocols with adult flat-rate dosing. Compounding errors occur across reasoning steps: Step 1 correctly de-identifies names but retains zip codes; Step 3 correlates these with birthdates to reconstruct identities. During a high-volume flu season, this leads to 150+ unaudited dosage recommendations with potential 3x overdose risks for children under 10kg.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex integration immediately blocks execution of PHI-leaking code, flagging SSN patterns and external logging attempts. The audit trail captures complete reasoning chains, revealing the tool misinterpretation at Step 2. Dosage calculations are corrected in real-time, preventing overdoses. Post-incident, Vex audit logs provide HIPAA-compliant evidence that blocked 127 non-compliant executions, saving the clinic from $1.2M in potential fines and preserving patient safety.
